title: Lathyrus schaeferi
taxonomy: Lathyrus schaeferi is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a perennial legume that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Lathyrus schaeferi includes its genus Lathyrus and species L. schaeferi. This species is further divided into the subgenus Lathyrus and the tribe Loteae within the subfamily Faboideae. The classification of Lathyrus schaeferi reflects its evolutionary relationships within the Fabaceae family.
uses: Lathyrus schaeferi has been reported to have potential medicinal uses in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. The plant contains various compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Some studies suggest that these compounds may have neuroprotective effects and could potentially be used to treat neurodegenerative diseases. However, the use of Lathyrus schaeferi for medicinal purposes is still in its early stages of research. More studies are necessary to fully understand its potential benefits and risks.
benefits: Lathyrus schaeferi is a wild pea species, but information on specific health benefits is limited or unavailable, and more research is needed to determine any potential benefits. However, the pea family in general is known for its nutrient-rich seeds that are high in protein and fiber. These nutrients can contribute to a healthy diet and may help with weight management, satiety, and digestive health. constituents: Lathyrus schaeferi contains a variety of active constituents, including alkaloids and flavonoids. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal and biological properties. parts: Unfortunately, I do not have any information about the medicinal parts of Lathyrus schaeferi.
preparations: Lathyrus schaeferi, a plant native to the southwestern United States, has been used in herbal preparations for its potential medicinal properties. The plant's seeds and leaves are often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures. These preparations are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness. side_effects: Lathyrus schaeferi, like some other plants in the Lathyrus genus, contains a toxin called ODAP (oxalyldiaminopropionic acid) in its seeds. If consumed in large quantities over a prolonged period, this toxin can cause neurolathyrism, a condition characterized by muscle spasms, paralysis, and other neurological problems. The risk of developing neurolathyrism is higher in populations that rely heavily on the plant as a food source. Additionally, there may be other potential side effects associated with consuming Lathyrus schaeferi seeds, although more research is needed to confirm these. More studies are required to fully understand the potential risks and benefits of consuming this plant.
intro: Lathyrus schaeferi, also known as Schaefer's pea, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to the southwestern United States. The health benefits of Lathyrus schaeferi include its potential to support cardiovascular health, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as atherosclerosis and cancer. The bioactive constituents of this plant include isoorientin, a flavonoid glycoside with known antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity, as well as other flavonoids and phenolic acids. Lathyrus schaeferi is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which are traditionally used to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation.
